summary: This page concludes a list of basic system classifications, including noncausal versus causal, lumped-parameter versus distributed-parameter, continuous-time versus discrete-time, analog versus digital, and invertible versus noninvertible systems. It also contrasts internal and external system descriptions and notes limitations of external descriptions for uncontrollable or unobservable systems. The page ends with references and the beginning of end-of-chapter problems on signal energy.
